content.full_text:
April 16, 1992


Beverly J. Churchill
River Pines Boosters' Club, Inc.
Box 91
River Pines, CA 95675

                                        Re: Your Request for Advice
                                            Our File No. I-92-142

Dear Ms. Churchill:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding application of the mass mailing provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ to the River Pines Boosters Club
newsletter. The Commission provides advice to persons whose
duties under the Act are in question or to that person's
authorized representative. The River Pines Boosters Club is a
private organization and is not subject to the provisions of the
Act. However, because your club is involved with a public
entity's mass mailing, and the public entity is subject to the
provisions of the Act, we provide you with a discussion, in
general terms, of the requirements of the Act. (Regulation
18329(c)(4).)²

                                        FACTS

     The River Pines Boosters Club, Inc. is a nonprofit
organization formed in 1964 for the improvement and betterment of
the River Pines community. The River Pines Boosters Club is a

     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

     ² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-142
Page 2

non-political group and is not in any way connected with the River
Pines Public Utility District.

     The River Pines Boosters Club has entered into a contract
with the River Pines Public Utility District whereby the Booster
Club pays for one half of the cost of mailing the River Pines
Public Utility District monthly statements to residents in the
jurisdiction.  The Boosters Club newsletter is included in the
same envelope and is mailed to the residents' homes along with the
monthly statements.

                              ANALYSIS

     As you were advised during the course of our telephone
conversation, the Act prohibits mass mailings at public expense.
(Section 89001.)  A mass mailing is two hundred substantially
similar pieces of mail, but does not include a form letter or
other mail which is sent in response to an unsolicited request,
letter or other inquiry.  (Section 82041.5.)  Accordingly, the
monthly statements prepared and sent by the River Pines Public
Utility District constitute a mass mailing if more than 200
monthly statements are sent out to the residents of River Pines.

     To clarify the provisions of Section 89001, the Commission
has enacted Regulation 18901.  This regulation provides, in
pertinent part, that a mass mailing sent to the recipient at his
or her residence is prohibited if the item sent either "features"
an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or includes the name, office, photograph, or
other reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent in
cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.  (Regulation 18901(a)(2).)"

     The monthly billing statement which is mailed to the
residents of the River Pines Public Utility District is prepared
and sent at public expense.  Accordingly, any material included in
the mailing must comply with the mass mailing requirements of the
Act.  Therefore, if the River Pines Boosters Club newsletter is
mailed in conjunction with the public utility district's monthly
billing, the newsletter must not contain articles featuring
elected officials of the district, photographs of the officials,
or any other matter prohibited under the terms of Regulation
18901.




3
___________________________
     We have previously provided you with a copy of Regulation 18901
for your reference.

Our File No. I-92-142
Page 3


We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.4

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]
By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:bb


4     Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Can the River Pines Boosters Club newsletter be included in the River Pines Public Utility District's mass mailing of monthly billing statements without violating the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The newsletter may be included in the utility district's mass mailing only if it complies with Regulation 18901 and does not feature elected officials, include their photographs, names, or other references, or be prepared in coordination with elected officers of the district.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Informal advice letter (no immunity provided); numbered file 1-92-142; no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but clear advisory structure with FACTS and ANALYSIS sections; document discusses mass mailing requirements and Regulation 18901 compliance

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the River Pines Boosters Club newsletter be included in the River Pines Public Utility District's mass mailing of monthly billing statements without violating the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions?

CONCLUSION: The newsletter may be included in the utility district's mass mailing only if it complies with Regulation 18901 and does not feature elected officials, include their photographs, names, or other references, or be prepared in coordination with elected officers of the district.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the River Pines Boosters Club that while the club itself is not subject to the Political Reform Act, its newsletter included in a public utility district's mass mailing must comply with the Act's prohibitions on featuring elected officials in mass mailings sent at public expense.
